rationale: Title Y is stronger overall because it states the key clinical result, gives the 96% same-day discharge figure, and includes the comparative time-to-discharge finding. It loses some journal-fit and clarity points for length and slightly causal wording, but the added specificity is valuable for Europace readers.
loser_weakness: Title X is clean and journal-appropriate, but it reads more like a study topic than a result-bearing title. It does not communicate the main finding or effect size.
